data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T062603-3643ecf5/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T062603-3643ecf5/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T062603-3643ecf5/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T062603-3643ecf5/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T062603-3643ecf5/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="fizeau-1e5bcee1">
    <title>Lint rule: forbid external imports of per-harness packages outside execute_dispatch.go</title>
    <description>
Source manifest: .ddx/beads-harness-interface-refactor.yaml
Source bead ID: BEAD-HARNESS-IF-10
Governing plan: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-014-universal-harness-interface.md

See the manifest and the plan for full context, touch-point inventory, and sub-sequence ordering.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. internal/lint/ (or the project lint pass) rejects any .go file outside internal/harnesses/ that imports internal/harnesses/{claude,codex,gemini,opencode,pi} except internal/serviceimpl/execute_dispatch.go
2. Rule wired into lefthook.yml and CI
3. Error message cites CONTRACT-004 invariant #1
4. An intentional violation in a feature branch reproduces the failure with a clear message
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:harness, kind:refactor, spec:CONTRACT-004, area:lint, ac-quality:needs-refinement</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="ADR-014" base-rev="516ceac0a06420891225da52b53e26ce0c61a290" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T062603-3643ecf5"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="ADR-014" path="docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-014-universal-harness-interface.md">ADR-014: Universal Harness Interface</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed as `aeba3b87` with `chore: enforce harness import boundary [fizeau-1e5bcee1]`.

The change adds `internal/lint/harnessimports`, which scans repo Go imports and fails on any direct import of `internal/harnesses/{claude,codex,gemini,opencode,pi}` outside `internal/harnesses/*` and `internal/serviceimpl/execute_dispatch.go`, with an error message that cites `CONTRACT-004 invariant #1`. The existing external import sites were removed by routing runner construction through [internal/harnesses/builtin/registry.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-1e5bcee1-20260515T062603-3643ecf5/internal/harnesses/builtin/registry.go), and the rule is wired into [Makefile](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-1e5bcee1-20260515T062603-3643ecf5/Makefile), [lefthook.yml](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-1e5bcee1-20260515T062603-3643ecf5/lefthook.yml), and [ci.yml](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-1e5bcee1-20260515T062603-3643ecf5/.github/workflows/ci.yml). The intentional-violation reproduction is covered by `TestScanFlagsExternalHarnessImport`, which creates a fake feature-branch file and asserts the clear failure message.

Verification passed with `make lint`, `make vet`, and `go test -count=1 ./...`.
